name: Mist Wolf
description: A lean spirit beast with pale fur and eyes like wet jade.

phase.announcement: The mist wolf stops pressing forward. It circles at distance, fur raised, one leg not fully bearing weight. Wounded animals with nothing left to lose are the most dangerous kind.
attackLines: The wolf lunges with desperate speed — {damage} damage. It is not fighting with skill now. It is fighting with fear.
A wild snap catches you before you can track the movement. {damage} damage. Wounded, it has stopped being predictable.
The wolf pivots and strikes low from the side. {damage} damage — you did not see that angle until it was already committed.

name: Road Bandits
description: Hungry mortals with stolen spears, frightened eyes, and orders from someone they dare not name.

phase.announcement: Two of the bandits break and run. The last one standing holds his spear differently now — not with training, but with the focus of someone who has nowhere to retreat. He is more afraid of whoever sent him than he is of you.
attackLines: He thrusts with nothing held back — {damage} damage. Desperation has stripped out the hesitation.
The spear comes low and fast. {damage} damage. He has stopped thinking about form.
He catches you in the exchange for {damage} damage. Whatever fear was slowing him before has converted into something else.

name: Spirit Boar
description: A heavyset beast with dark bristles and small eyes that carry more intelligence than they should. Outer disciples avoid its territory during the first week of each month.

phase.announcement: The boar stops circling. It drops its head and its breathing changes — slower, heavier. You have seen this before in cornered animals. It is not preparing to flee. It is preparing to charge through you.
attackLines: The boar charges in a straight line, no feinting. {damage} damage — pure mass and speed before you can fully redirect.
It swings its tusks low and catches your leg for {damage} damage. The wound is not deep, but the force behind it is honest.
The spirit qi in its bristles sparks as it slams forward. {damage} damage. Whatever calm it had before is gone now.
It pivots faster than something this size should and catches your side. {damage} damage — you underestimated how quickly it could turn.

name: Shadow Viper
description: A finger-thick serpent the colour of dried leaves. It does not announce itself. The only warning is the gap in the undergrowth where there was not one before.

phase.announcement: The viper draws into a tight coil, head low, tongue tasting the air every half-second. Injured vipers are the reason sect healers keep anti-venom at the ready. The snake is not retreating. It is recalculating.
attackLines: It strikes twice in succession — you block the first and the second catches your wrist for {damage} damage. Fast is its only language.
The viper uncoils at full extension, hitting you before the motion was readable. {damage} damage from an angle you did not track.
It drives for your neck. You turn your shoulder into it. {damage} damage anyway — at this range, partial blocks are the best you can manage.

name: Cloud Stag
description: A pale-antlered stag that treads without sound through the upper grove. Its breath fogs even in summer. Killing one is considered wasteful by most outer disciples.

phase.announcement: The stag raises its head and sounds once — a low resonant call that echoes further than it should. Then it turns to face you directly, antlers lowered, and the qi in its horns brightens like cold light before a storm. It has stopped fleeing.
attackLines: The antlers sweep in a wide arc — {damage} damage. The qi discharge along the tips adds more force than the strike alone could carry.
It strikes forward and drives you back with {damage} damage. There is something deliberate in the motion that was not there before.
Cloud qi pulses down its flanks and it charges. {damage} damage — you brace and still lose ground.
Its antler tip grazes your arm before you clear the swing. {damage} damage from something that moved faster than a creature this size should.

name: Stone Crab
description: A crab the size of a medium dog with a shell like river-worn granite. It moves slowly until it does not.

phase.announcement: The stone crab retracts its legs and slams its shell against the ground. The impact vibrates through the stone underfoot. For a moment it is motionless — then the shell fractures at the joints and it emerges lower, faster, and visibly angrier.
attackLines: A claw catches you before your guard is ready. {damage} damage — the grip strength behind it is not something you measured in time.
It moves sideways and snaps low. {damage} damage. At this height, blocking costs more than taking the blow.
The fractured shell reveals something meaner underneath. It drives forward for {damage} damage — slower creatures cut corners when they're hurt.
A claw clamps around your forearm and wrenches before releasing. {damage} damage. The bruise will take a week.

name: Bandit Scout
description: A lean figure who moves like someone trained to not be seen. He carries a short knife and the particular caution of a man who survives by knowing when to run.

phase.announcement: The scout glances toward the tree line. He is weighing something. Then he decides to stay — which tells you either he cannot leave, or what is behind him is worse than you are.
attackLines: He slashes fast and low with the knife — {damage} damage. No stance, no form. Just the motion of someone who has done this before.
He feints left and comes right. {damage} damage. Desperation has made him harder to read, not easier.
The knife catches you in the exchange for {damage} damage. He is no fighter, but he has survived long enough to be dangerous.

name: Bandit Lieutenant
description: A broad-shouldered man with a road-forged shortsword and the air of someone who has given orders long enough to believe they mean something.

phase.announcement: The lieutenant drops the commanding posture. He rolls his shoulder, adjusts his grip, and his expression closes into something flat. This is the version of him that kept his position. He does not shout orders now.
attackLines: A quick combination — feint high, cut low. {damage} damage before you finish your counter.
He drives forward without setup. {damage} damage. The aggression is a choice, not a panic.
The shortsword comes across in a controlled arc. {damage} damage — this is someone who has practised this motion ten thousand times.
He uses his shoulder to disrupt your stance and follows with the blade. {damage} damage. Simple and effective.

name: Corrupt Outer Disciple
description: An outer disciple wearing a sect robe with the Azure Cloud insignia cut away. The eyes are wrong — flat and calculating where a cultivator's should have some depth.

phase.announcement: The disciple's hand moves to something under his robe. When it comes back out, the qi threading through his fingers is wrong — dark at the edges, like ink in water. Whatever method this is, the Azure Cloud Sect does not teach it.
attackLines: The dark qi in his strike carries more force than the motion alone. {damage} damage — you feel it settle somewhere beneath the surface.
He drives a palm forward laced with corrupt qi. {damage} damage. The impact leaves a cold residue you do not like the feel of.
Two quick strikes with the corrupted method — {damage} combined damage. He is burning through it faster than is wise, which tells you he has nothing left to save it for.
The corrupt qi flares as he hits. {damage} damage. You note the way it disperses and file it away.

name: Mist Shade
description: A spirit entity shaped like a person but with no weight behind the shape. It drifts through solid objects if it moves slowly enough. The touch carries a cold that has nothing to do with temperature.

phase.announcement: The mist shade fractures — for a moment there are two of it, overlapping and slightly out of sync. The halves pull back together, but something about the shape has changed. It is more present now, not less. The wounds you dealt appear to have made it more coherent, not scattered it.
attackLines: A cold hand reaches through your guard and grips something that does not have a name. {damage} damage — you cannot block what passes through solid matter.
The shade strikes from two angles simultaneously. One is a feint. You do not find out which until after. {damage} damage.
It phases through the stone beside you and strikes from inside your reach. {damage} damage. At this distance, the qi-cold carries further than the blow.
The shade's palm drives into your chest with a resonance that rattles your qi flow. {damage} damage — and your next movement feels heavier than it should.

name: Bone Hound
description: A spirit beast assembled from the remains of other creatures. The bones fit imperfectly, held together by a low persistent qi that is older than the formation that made it.

phase.announcement: A rib detaches from the hound's side and embeds in the stone wall beside you. The creature does not slow down. The bones reshuffle with a grinding sound — something from one part moving to reinforce another. What remains is smaller, leaner, and focused entirely on you.
attackLines: A bone from the reconstructed frame launches ahead of the lunge — {damage} damage arrives before you see the body coming.
The hound's jaw closes around your forearm for {damage} damage — the grip is mechanical, not alive, and it does not tire.
It drives its remaining mass straight forward. {damage} damage. The weight is less than before, but the qi behind it is concentrated.
The bone frame shifts mid-strike, changing the angle. {damage} damage. Patterns that worked before are no longer accurate.

name: Black-Thread Cultist
description: A robed figure whose hands are traced with black qi-thread tattoos that move faintly when still. He speaks like someone reciting rather than choosing words.

phase.announcement: The cultist pulls his outer robe back over his shoulders. The black thread tattoos are running now — moving up his neck, branching across his collarbone. He was holding back. Whatever method this is, it operates on something other than ordinary qi pathways. The temperature in the immediate area drops several degrees.
attackLines: Black thread qi lashes out in a wide arc — {damage} damage across multiple points simultaneously. It is not a strike so much as a net.
The cultist drives a condensed thread directly into your qi channels. {damage} damage, and your qi feels sluggish for the next exchange.
He moves with the threads amplifying every joint — faster than his size suggests. {damage} damage before you close the defensive gap.
A thread anchors to the ground and the cultist uses it as a pivot to strike from an unexpected angle. {damage} damage. The method has more dimensions to it than you have seen.
The black qi threads tighten into a single concentrated blow. {damage} damage — the most honest attack he has made. The technique is no longer being conserved.
